title: Mirabilis gigantea
intro: Mirabilis gigantea, commonly known as the giant four o'clock, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Four O'Clock family (Nyctaginaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Mirabilis gigantea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Mirabilis gigantea is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
taxonomy: Mirabilis gigantea is a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. It is native to the Americas and belongs to the genus Mirabilis, which includes several other species of four o'clock flowers. The Nyctaginaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the Americas, with some species in Africa and Asia. The family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Nyctagineae being one of the major subfamilies. Mirabilis gigantea is a unique species within its genus due to its large size and showy flowers.
